skill.name: form-cro
skill.description: Optimize lead capture, contact, demo request, application, survey, checkout, and quote request forms for higher completion rates.
skill.summary: Audits non-signup forms and recommends field, layout, copy, validation, and testing improvements.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed in context and dismissed as false positives. The reported backticks are Markdown fences, weak cryptography hits are ordinary form optimization text, and reconnaissance hits are email and error-message examples.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Lines 123 to 131 contain Markdown examples for good and bad form placeholders. They do not invoke a shell, execute commands, or process user-controlled input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static hits are triple backtick Markdown fences around text examples. No executable command, interpreter call, or shell syntax is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography findings occur in descriptive marketing and form optimization text. No hashing algorithm, encryption API, key handling, or cryptographic operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are headings, metadata, or guidance about mobile, design, and form copy. There is no security-sensitive implementation context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance findings are user-facing form guidance about email typo detection and validation messages. They do not collect host data, environment details, files, or network configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is validation copy for forms, not system inspection. The examples mention an email domain and invalid input only.

content.value_statement: Forms often lose users through unnecessary fields, unclear copy, and weak validation. This skill provides focused CRO audits and practical recommendations for higher completion rates.
actual_capabilities: Audits non-signup forms for field count, order, labels, validation, and friction.
Recommends required and optional fields with business rationale.
Suggests button copy, trust elements, mobile layout, and error-message improvements.
Creates A/B test hypotheses for form structure, fields, copy, and UX changes.
Adapts guidance for lead capture, contact, demo, quote, survey, checkout, and application forms.
limitations: Does not replace analytics data, session recordings, or controlled experiments.
Does not handle signup or registration flows, which need a separate skill.
Does not implement frontend code or connect analytics tools by itself.
Requires context about form goals and required fields for the best recommendations.

use_cases.title: Improve Lead Form Completion
use_cases.description: Review gated content or newsletter forms and reduce unnecessary friction before launch.

use_cases.title: Audit Demo Request Forms
use_cases.description: Balance sales qualification needs with a simpler path to request a demo.

use_cases.title: Fix Checkout or Quote Forms
use_cases.description: Identify field, layout, validation, and mobile issues that may reduce submissions.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Form Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this form for conversion issues. The form type is [type], the target user is [audience], and the current fields are [fields].

prompt_templates.title: Field Reduction Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Prioritize which fields to keep, remove, or make optional
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these form fields and recommend required, optional, and removed fields. Explain the business tradeoff for each change.

prompt_templates.title: Mobile Form Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Optimize this form for mobile users. Focus on field order, keyboard types, touch targets, validation, and submit button placement.

prompt_templates.title: A/B Test Roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a prioritized A/B testing roadmap for this form. Include hypotheses, expected impact, required tracking, and success metrics.

output_examples.input: A demo form asks for name, email, phone, company size, budget, timeline, and a required message.
output: High priority: Make phone and message optional unless sales follow-up requires them.
Medium priority: Move budget and timeline after the core contact fields or use conditional questions.
Test idea: Compare the current form against a shorter version with name, email, company, and goal.

output_examples.input: A contact form has vague labels and shows Invalid input after submission errors.
output: Use visible labels and examples that stay available during typing.
Replace generic errors with field-specific messages that explain how to fix the problem.
Preserve entered values after errors and focus the first field that needs attention.

output_examples.input: A lead capture form gates a guide and asks for six business details.
output: Start with email and name, then ask enrichment questions after the download.
Place the value proposition above the form and clarify what the user receives.
Measure form start rate, completion rate, field drop-off, and mobile performance.

best_practices: Provide the form goal, target user, field list, and current completion rate when available.
Separate legally required fields from fields that are only useful for follow-up.
Validate recommendations with field-level analytics and controlled tests.
anti_patterns: Using the skill for signup or registration flows instead of general forms.
Removing fields that compliance, fulfillment, or qualification truly requires.
Treating estimated conversion impact as proof without running a test.

faq.question: What forms does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers lead capture, contact, demo request, application, survey, checkout, and quote request forms.

faq.question: Does it cover signup or registration forms?
faq.answer: No. Signup and registration flows need separate analysis because account creation has different friction and trust issues.

faq.question: Can it recommend exact fields to remove?
faq.answer: Yes. It can recommend fields to keep, remove, or make optional when you provide the form goal and field list.

faq.question: Can it create A/B test ideas?
faq.answer: Yes. It can propose test hypotheses for structure, fields, copy, trust elements, validation, and mobile UX.

faq.question: Does it need analytics data?
faq.answer: Analytics data improves the recommendations, but the skill can still perform a qualitative review without it.

faq.question: Will it implement form changes?
faq.answer: No. It provides CRO recommendations and test ideas, but implementation requires a design or development workflow.
